metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3711
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Expression of dominant-negative WAVE2 proline-rich region decreases spine density and size in primary hippocampal neurons (14 DIV).

30/8/2017 Pim
- No experimental reference made to actin and WAVE2 made. Is implied by the authors in results and discussion.. Experimental description: 25/9/2017 Pim
- "Cultured hippocampal neurons were prepared from embryonic day 18 (E18) rat brain as described previously"
- Species of dom.neg. WAVE2 not stated in the paper. Authors contacted in email dd. 25/9/2017 (kime@kaist.ac.kr). Kim replied:

"Dear Pim van Nierop,

Thanks much for your message.

I contacted the first author of the paper, Jeonghoon Choi, who is now in USA, and heard that it is highly likely to be mouse rather than rat. But he was not 100% sure, although mice and rats share 100% aa identity in this segment of the WAVE protein. If you want, we can try to find the cDNA and sequence it. I am very sorry for not being able to be more clear at this moment.

Best wishes,
Eunjoon"

And:

"Dear Pim,

Thanks for your message. I guess we are more comfortable with mouse because it is much more likely to be mouse (i.e. > 90%).

Best wishes,
Eunjoon"

- Decission: I have selected mouse.
